content.full_text:
WILLIAM M VEGA

Dear Mr. Luis Hanuman- FPPC

As Hanuman per our phone conversation
I'm requesting an opinion letter. I'm
a candidate for city council in the city of
BREA.
    Does my mothers ownership of property
in the city of BREA create a conflict of
interest for me if i were to be elected
to the city council.

I would appreciate a response as soon
as possible. Thank you


Sincerely


William M Vega

659 BEULWOOD DR.
BREA, CA. 92621
PH. (310) 694-3589 OFFICE
P.P (714) 671-0146 HOME.

SEP 25 '92 10:20                PAGE .002

sections.question: Does my mother's ownership of property in the City of Brea create a conflict of interest for me if I were to be elected to the City Council?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester, a candidate for city council in Brea, asks whether his mother's property ownership in the city would create a conflict of interest if he is elected.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal question/conclusion sections are present. The document contains the requester's name, address, and phone contact information. Request appears to relate to Government Code conflict of interest provisions.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Does my mother's ownership of property in the City of Brea create a conflict of interest for me if I were to be elected to the City Council?
embedding.first_500_words: WILLIAM M VEGA Dear Mr. Luis Hanuman- FPPC As Hanuman per our phone conversation I'm requesting an opinion letter. I'm a candidate for city council in the city of BREA. Does my mothers ownership of property in the city of BREA create a conflict of interest for me if i were to be elected to the city council. I would appreciate a response as soon as possible. Thank you Sincerely William M Vega 659 BEULWOOD DR. BREA, CA. 92621 PH. (310) 694-3589 OFFICE P.P (714) 671-0146 HOME. SEP 25 '92 10:20 PAGE .002
embedding.summary: William M. Vega, a candidate for Brea City Council, requests an FPPC opinion letter regarding whether his mother's property ownership in the city would constitute a conflict of interest for him as a council member.
